concept: Modular exponentiation; the discrete logarithm problem
label: Discrete logarithm problem
assumed_background: commit/open/verify as the PCS interface from the previous chapter
learning_objectives: Define the discrete logarithm problem in a prime-order cyclic group.
Distinguish fast exponentiation from brute-force recovery in a toy group.
Explain why the hardness assumption is conditional rather than a theorem.
graphify_contribution: Start node was Discrete Logarithm Problem; used it as supporting context for DLog-based cryptosystems and Shor vulnerability, not for extra prerequisites.
